channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Health officials confirm tuberculosis case at Colorado ICE facility; ICE denies any active cases
item.description: Health officials say the facility has not provided "the information or access necessary" to complete the investigation and determine if there are additional cases.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Biden memoir set for November release amid debate over his legacy
item.description: “Promise Me, America,” Joe Biden’s new memoir, will revisit his presidency and decision to end his 2024 reelection campaign.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Faint new planet is revealed around a young star after a decade in hiding
item.description: Astronomers have discovered a faint, elusive planet orbiting a young star after more than a decade of cosmic hide-and-seek.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Taco Bell removes some ingredients during nationwide cyclospora investigation
item.description: Taco Bell removed select ingredients at some locations as health officials investigate a nationwide surge in cyclospora infections.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Pinellas Schools seeks public input on next phase of campus closures, consolidations
item.description: The Pinellas County School District is asking parents, staff and residents to help shape the district's next round of school closure and consolidation recommendations as leaders continue to respond to falling student enrollment and the rising cost of maintaining older campuses.

item.title: [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Westcoast Black Theatre Troupe debuts new Marvin Gaye revue ahead of international festival
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Parrish Grandma Club returns for 2026-2027 season

[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] How strong have prior Berthas been and when did they develop?

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Trump pushes ICE to resume traffic stops after deadly incidents
item.description: Trump defended ICE traffic stops as a key enforcement tool after DHS temporarily halted most stops following two fatal shootings.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Senate to question Todd Blanche as he seeks permanent attorney general role
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche faces a Senate confirmation hearing as Democrats press him on DOJ transparency and Republicans rally behind him.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Mastodons and manatees at Bradenton's Bishop Museum of Science and Nature
item.description: Mastodons and manatees at Bradenton's Bishop Museum of Science and Nature. The downtown waterfront attraction has an indoor "backyard" play zone just for kids.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Susan Solves It: Credit card costs
item.description: Tampa Bay 28 Reporter Susan El Khoury advises paying off rewards credit cards in full each month to avoid interest charges that can outweigh the benefits.

item.title: [Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] Pinellas schools ordered to drop St. Petersburg Catholic from all schedules
[ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] Condo building damaged in fire on Longboat Key
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Search underway for missing Tampa woman, 27
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Is the 'outdated' clock change ending? Daylight saving's status
[WATCHLIST: hurricane] [Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Hurricane Center identifies area in the Gulf for possible development
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Former Florida cop to be executed same day as 80-year-old man
[WATCHLIST: hurricane] [The Florida Channel] 7/14/26 Capitol Update Extended: State CFO Warns Floridians About Hurricane Season Scams
item.description: 7/14/26 Capitol Update Extended: State CFO Warns Floridians About Hurricane Season Scams first appeared on The Florida Channel CFO Blaise Ingoglia advised Floridians to be cautious about scams to steal personal information that occur during hurricane season. Hurricane season runs through November 30. 7/14/26 Capitol Update Extended: State CFO Warns Floridians About Hurricane Season Scams first app

item.title: [City of Tampa] New Affordable Units in East Tampa to Serve Those at Risk of Homelessness

[Pinellas County Sheriff] Spanish For Law Enforcement Graduation Ceremony (26-067) The Pinellas County Sheriff’s Office is recognizing members who have successfully completed Spanish for Law Enforcement Levels I and II.

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Death investigation underway at North Port apartment complex: NPPD
item.description: The North Port Police Department (NPPD) said it is conducting a death investigation at an appartment complex on Wednesday morning.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] News to Know for July 15
item.description: Pinellas schools ban smart glasses, tighten e-bike rules for students. Rays slugger Junior Caminero feels fortunate left hand not broken after he was hit in All-Star Game.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Life after war: A veteran’s journey to find relief in ketamine therapy as program faces cuts
item.description: A Florida-funded study is examining whether ketamine can help veterans struggling with mental health challenges, but advocates say the program's future is uncertain.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] The small engine part making oil changes more expensive
item.description: Drivers of many newer vehicles are being hit with a surcharge at oil change time, because the little plug at the bottom of their car's engine can no longer be reused. Why is this happening?

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tehran threatens to halt all Mideast energy exports after US reimposes its blockade on Iran
item.description: The U.S. reimposed a naval blockade on Iran and intensified its airstrike campaign early Wednesday in retaliation for Tehran’s attacks on ships trying to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Tampa man get 40 years for lighting man on fire at gas station: prosecutors
item.description: A Tampa man received 40 years in prison on Tuesday for lighting a man on fire at a gas station after an argument in 2023, prosecutors said.
